title: osmundales
intro: The osmundales is an order of ferns in the division Pteridophyta, characterized by its unique characteristics such as the presence of sporangia on the underside of leaves. The Osmandales order includes various families of ferns that are adapted to different environments. Families within this order include the Cyperaceae, which are typically found in wetland environments, and the Euphorbiaceae, which are generally found in a variety of habitats. However, the Orchidaceae, which is primarily known as an order of flowering plants, is not a part of the Osmandales order.

families.family_desc: Osmundaceae is a family of vascular plants commonly known as the royal ferns, consisting of about 18 species of ferns found primarily in the Northern Hemisphere.
